title: Viva Lost Wages

jokes.text: What, the static from Uncle Phil's corduroys?
jokes.setup: Carlton asks what generates natural power, expecting serious answer
jokes.punchline: Will suggests Uncle Phil's corduroy static as a power source
jokes.explanation: Perfect character-specific humor that plays on Uncle Phil's weight and formal dress style. Economical and unexpected.

jokes.text: I'm getting goose bumps.
jokes.setup: Carlton excitedly gives Will a book about Hoover Dam
jokes.punchline: Will sarcastically expresses fake excitement
jokes.explanation: Simple sarcastic response that establishes Will's disinterest in Carlton's educational tourism

jokes.text: There's certainly some places in this desert where a brother can become a man.
jokes.setup: Uncle Phil describes Native American manhood ritual in desert
jokes.punchline: Will suggests Vegas has adult entertainment venues
jokes.explanation: Will cleverly subverts Uncle Phil's noble desert vision with sexual innuendo about Vegas strip clubs

jokes.text: I don't know where none of them at, you know, you know, but... I'm gonna go ahead and get my knife.
jokes.setup: Will just made innuendo about adult venues in Vegas
jokes.punchline: Claims ignorance then eagerly prepares for 'ritual'
jokes.explanation: Will maintains plausible deniability while showing obvious eagerness. Good character-consistent humor.

jokes.text: Right now, they're in phase one. Chick flicks and mint Milanos.
jokes.setup: Uncle Phil complains the women aren't listening to his goodbye
jokes.punchline: Geoffrey clinically categorizes women's weekend behavior
jokes.explanation: Geoffrey's dry, analytical approach to female bonding rituals is perfectly in character and well-observed

jokes.text: I just know that one day Thelma and Louise will make it to the other side of the canyon.
jokes.setup: Uncle Phil says he's not going off to war
jokes.punchline: Geoffrey references tragic ending of famous female road movie
jokes.explanation: Geoffrey's optimistic reinterpretation of a famously tragic ending shows his dry wit

jokes.text: Oh, Auntie Em, Dorothy got a miniskirt on.
jokes.setup: Carlton says they're not in Kansas anymore (Wizard of Oz reference)
jokes.punchline: Will continues the reference noting Vegas showgirl costumes
jokes.explanation: Solid callback continuation that modernizes/sexualizes the classic reference

jokes.text: You're an odd little man, aren't you? Yeah.
jokes.setup: Carlton excitedly talks about mauve sunrise at Hoover Dam
jokes.punchline: Will calls him odd and Carlton immediately agrees
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment - Carlton's instant self-aware agreement is both funny and endearing

jokes.text: See, she can't believe it either.
jokes.setup: Carlton thinks cashier is shocked by Will's $100 gambling budget
jokes.punchline: Will suggests she's shocked by Carlton's cheapness instead
jokes.explanation: Will reframes the situation to mock Carlton's conservative approach to gambling

jokes.text: I eat around it, because cockroaches go straight to my thighs.
jokes.setup: Trivial Pursuit question about finding cockroach in salad
jokes.punchline: Hilary applies her diet obsession to pest control
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly captures Hilary's vanity by treating cockroaches like calories. Perfect character integration.

jokes.text: I'd switch plates with Carlton.
jokes.setup: Same cockroach-in-salad question
jokes.punchline: Ashley would trick Carlton into eating the bug
jokes.explanation: Shows Ashley's mischievous side and sibling rivalry with Carlton

jokes.text: Does it say what I'm wearing?
jokes.setup: Question about body being abandoned in woods after death
jokes.punchline: Hilary only cares about her outfit choice for corpse
jokes.explanation: Another perfect Hilary moment - her vanity transcends even death scenarios

jokes.text: It's just that I've never seen you three without your makeup before.
jokes.setup: Geoffrey apologizes for scaring the women
jokes.punchline: Reveals they look scary without makeup
jokes.explanation: Standard 'women without makeup' joke elevated slightly by Geoffrey's dry delivery

jokes.text: Miss Ashley, if it hasn't worked by now...
jokes.setup: Ashley asks if Geoffrey would use voodoo doll to hurt someone
jokes.punchline: Geoffrey implies he's already been trying to hurt someone
jokes.explanation: Geoffrey's dark humor suggests ongoing attempts at supernatural revenge - very in character

jokes.text: Like Mickey, only without the big white gloves.
jokes.setup: Ashley describes seeing a mouse
jokes.punchline: References Mickey Mouse but notes missing cartoon elements
jokes.explanation: Simple visual comparison joke that works but isn't particularly memorable

jokes.text: Give me the loot. Give me the loot. I'm a bad, bad man.
jokes.setup: Will wins at craps table
jokes.punchline: Will celebrates with gangster-style bravado
jokes.explanation: Will's over-the-top celebration fits his personality and the gambling high

jokes.text: Maybe so, but I can't lose either. Not unless it comes up... Double zero, green.
jokes.setup: Carlton bets on both red and black to avoid losing
jokes.punchline: Ball immediately lands on the one outcome that beats both bets
jokes.explanation: Classic ironic reversal - Carlton's 'foolproof' plan immediately backfires in the exact way he mentions

jokes.text: No, better not take the chance.
jokes.setup: Carlton is counting pennies to gamble with
jokes.punchline: Decides even penny gambling is too risky
jokes.explanation: Perfect Carlton moment showing his extreme risk aversion even with minimal stakes

jokes.text: I was like a crap Mandingo.
jokes.setup: Will describes his gambling prowess at the craps table
jokes.punchline: Compares himself to legendary fighter/lover using gambling terminology
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay combining 'crap' (dice game) with Mandingo reference - very Will-like braggadocio

jokes.text: Hey, can you spot a brother 20?
jokes.setup: Will just bragged about his incredible winning streak
jokes.punchline: Immediately asks Carlton for money, revealing he lost everything
jokes.explanation: Perfect puncturing of Will's bragging - the immediate deflation is expertly structured

jokes.text: Hi, my name is Will Smith, and I'm a gambleholic.
jokes.setup: Carlton confronts Will about his gambling problem
jokes.punchline: Will parodies AA meeting introductions
jokes.explanation: Standard addiction parody format but gets points for Will using his real name

jokes.text: I'm about to have me some of the devil's eggs and bacon too. Ha!
jokes.setup: Carlton warns about 'devil's juice' (gambling addiction)
jokes.punchline: Will extends metaphor to innocent breakfast foods
jokes.explanation: Will deflects Carlton's concern by playfully extending the 'devil' metaphor to harmless things

jokes.text: I don't wanna see no damn Hoover Dam.
jokes.setup: Carlton asks if Will wants to miss Hoover Dam
jokes.punchline: Will uses 'damn' as both profanity and part of dam's name
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective wordplay that emphasizes Will's frustration with Carlton's tourism

jokes.text: Carlton, what the hell are you doing? What am I doing? I'm gambling. What do you think? Come on, eight.
jokes.setup: Carlton was previously ultra-conservative about gambling
jokes.punchline: Carlton is now obsessively gambling and stating it like it's obvious
jokes.explanation: Great character reversal - Carlton's transformation from cautious to addicted gambler

jokes.text: I love you, man. You know I always loved you, man.
jokes.setup: Carlton wins a bet and gets excited
jokes.punchline: Carlton declares love for Will in gambling-induced euphoria
jokes.explanation: Shows Carlton's gambling high making him overly emotional - fits his personality swing

jokes.text: A word to the wise, Will, never leave your PIN number in your wallet.
jokes.setup: Carlton confesses to stealing from Will's ATM card
jokes.punchline: Carlton gives Will advice on preventing what Carlton just did
jokes.explanation: Perfect ironic humor - Carlton offers security advice after committing the crime himself

jokes.text: No, wait, wait. Wait a minute. It was 18, we had eight. We should get something.
jokes.setup: Carlton loses his final bet when 18 comes up instead of 8
jokes.punchline: Carlton thinks he should get partial credit for being close
jokes.explanation: Shows Carlton's desperate rationalization and misunderstanding of how gambling works

jokes.text: No, wait, wait. Wait a minute. It was 18, we had eight. We should get something.
jokes.setup: Carlton loses at roulette when 18 comes up instead of 8
jokes.punchline: Carlton thinks they should get partial credit for having one of the digits
jokes.explanation: Shows Carlton's naive understanding of gambling - thinking partial matches count. Perfect character logic.

jokes.text: Carlton. Carlton. And then the blackjack table was saying, 'You can do it.' And then the slot machines just started yelling: 'Hey, you tall handsome guy, come shake my hand.'
jokes.setup: Carlton explains why he couldn't stop gambling
jokes.punchline: Carlton anthropomorphizes casino games as seductive voices
jokes.explanation: Brilliant visualization of gambling addiction through Carlton's innocent perspective - the machines become friends

jokes.text: These are all my new friends.
jokes.setup: Carlton's delusional explanation about casino games calling to him
jokes.punchline: Refers to gambling machines as friends
jokes.explanation: Pathetic and funny - Carlton's social awkwardness extends to considering gambling machines as friends.

jokes.text: It's Saturday, the guys are at the gym.
jokes.setup: Ashley wants to go to the mall to look at guys
jokes.punchline: Vivian points out the obvious scheduling conflict
jokes.explanation: Simple logical response that deflates Ashley's plan with practical reasoning

jokes.text: Hey, Rocky, watch me pull a rabbit out of a hat.
jokes.setup: Carlton sees mounted animal heads in casino office
jokes.punchline: Carlton references Bullwinkle cartoon to mounted moose head
jokes.explanation: Carlton's innocent cartoon reference in intimidating casino office shows his naivety

jokes.text: Shut the hell up.
jokes.setup: Will explains his plan requires Carlton to follow instructions
jokes.punchline: Will's only instruction is for Carlton to be quiet
jokes.explanation: Will's blunt 'instruction' shows he knows Carlton will mess up any real plan

jokes.text: Too late. He said, 'Too late.' He put the ring up. You know, he said, 'Too late.'
jokes.setup: Casino boss dismisses Will's pitch to make him rich
jokes.punchline: Will awkwardly over-explains the obvious rejection
jokes.explanation: Will's nervous over-explanation of the casino boss's dismissal shows his desperation

jokes.text: Losing is more fun than winning.
jokes.setup: Will pitches casino boss on a new business model
jokes.punchline: Will's absurd central premise that people prefer losing
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly absurd premise that sounds almost logical in Will's desperate pitch

jokes.text: You deserve every one of them heads, Fred.
jokes.setup: Will trying to flatter the casino executive
jokes.punchline: Compliments him on his hunting trophies in a desperate attempt to bond
jokes.explanation: Will's desperate attempt to connect with the executive through his hunting trophies shows his desperation.

jokes.text: I've got space for you on my wall.
jokes.setup: Casino boss threatens to count to three
jokes.punchline: Implies he'll mount their heads with the other animal trophies
jokes.explanation: Dark but effective threat that references the mounted animal heads mentioned earlier

jokes.text: I don't think it's gonna be the shock that you think it is.
jokes.setup: Carlton worries about disappointing his father
jokes.punchline: Will suggests Uncle Phil already knows Carlton is a failure
jokes.explanation: Will's brutal honesty about Carlton's reputation with Uncle Phil

jokes.text: You're pathetic.
jokes.setup: Carlton gets excited about finding a quarter
jokes.punchline: Will's blunt assessment of Carlton's desperation
jokes.explanation: Direct, honest reaction to Carlton's gambling desperation, effective but simple.

jokes.text: Carlton, look, a quarter. Oh, it's mine. It's mine.
jokes.setup: Will points out quarter on ground
jokes.punchline: Carlton's immediate desperate claim of ownership
jokes.explanation: Carlton's gambling addiction so strong he claims random quarter - good desperation comedy.

jokes.text: Because there ain't no quarter, man.
jokes.setup: Carlton asks why Will can see the quarter he can't
jokes.punchline: Will reveals Carlton is hallucinating from gambling withdrawal
jokes.explanation: Reveals Carlton's gambling addiction has progressed to hallucinations - dark but funny

jokes.text: My mom got better moves than that the time her girdle slipped in Church.
jokes.setup: Will dismisses the dance competition
jokes.punchline: Will references his mother's embarrassing church wardrobe malfunction
jokes.explanation: Vivid, specific image that's both embarrassing and funny - very Will-like storytelling

jokes.text: Please welcome Will the Thrill and Boogaloo Shrimp.
jokes.setup: Dance contest introduction
jokes.punchline: Carlton gets ridiculous breakdancing nickname
jokes.explanation: The contrast between Carlton's uptight personality and street dance nickname is amusing

jokes.text: A very odd little man, but a man.
jokes.setup: Will reflects on Carlton coming through their Vegas ordeal
jokes.punchline: Will acknowledges Carlton's growth while maintaining he's still odd
jokes.explanation: Nice callback that shows character growth while maintaining the established dynamic

jokes.text: I mean, how dare they give us the booby prize.
jokes.setup: Carlton complaining about their dance contest result
jokes.punchline: Calls their prize money a 'booby prize' when it actually saved them
jokes.explanation: Carlton's inability to appreciate that their 'booby prize' actually solved their problem shows his continued cluelessness.

jokes.text: Oh, yeah? Well, I got 20 that says I can. Hey, Will, you want some action on this?
jokes.setup: Carlton just swore off gambling forever
jokes.punchline: Carlton immediately starts betting on birthday candles
jokes.explanation: Perfect ending that shows Carlton learned nothing - immediate relapse into gambling addiction

tmdb_overview: Carlton is on a roll in Vegas, celebrating his birthday at the gaming tables with Will in tow but suddenly the tables turn, and the duo enter a dance contest to recoup their losses.
